meta.name: Chicken Shawarma
meta.subtitle: Spiced yogurt-marinated chicken, roasted or grilled, with classic shawarma flavors

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Designed for oven-first households (no rotisserie required) with optional grill/air-fryer paths.
Includes SSA-friendly ingredient IDs and planner signals.

summary: Juicy chicken marinated with yogurt, lemon, garlic, and warm shawarma spices, then roasted (or grilled) and sliced for wraps, bowls, or plates.

yield.notes: About 2.5–3 lb chicken yields 6 hearty servings (wraps/bowls) or 8 lighter servings.

heat.note: Add cayenne or extra black pepper for more heat.

optional.label: Cast-iron skillet
optional.notes: Great for searing strips quickly after roasting.

optional.label: Grill
optional.notes: Adds extra smoke/char. Works best with thighs.

notes: Contains dairy (yogurt). Use dairy-free yogurt for a dairy-free adaptation.

ingredients.label: Boneless skinless chicken thighs
ingredients.notes: Best flavor/juiciness. Substitute chicken breast, but reduce cook time and avoid overcooking.

ingredients.label: Plain yogurt (whole milk preferred)
ingredients.notes: Greek yogurt works; thin with 1–2 tbsp water if very thick.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust if using fine salt or if serving with salty sauces/pickles.

ingredients.label: Ground allspice
ingredients.notes: If you don't have allspice, use a pinch of clove + extra cinnamon.

ingredients.label: Ground cardamom
ingredients.notes: Optional but very authentic; keep light.

ingredients.label: Yellow onion, thinly sliced
ingredients.notes: Optional for roasting alongside chicken; adds sweetness.

ingredients.label: Garlic sauce (toum) or garlic mayo (to serve)

steps.title: Mix the shawarma marinade
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together yogurt, lemon juice, garlic, olive oil, salt, black pepper, cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, cinnamon, and allspice.
Add optional cardamom and cayenne if using.
Taste the marinade (it should be boldly seasoned). Adjust salt/lemon if needed.
checks: Marinade is smooth and evenly mixed (no spice clumps).
Flavor is strong—marinade should taste slightly over-seasoned before cooking.

steps.title: Coat and marinate the chicken
instructions: Add chicken thighs to the bowl and toss until every piece is well coated.
Cover and refrigerate at least 30 minutes (best: 4–12 hours).
If short on time, marinate while the oven preheats and you prep sides.
checks: Chicken is fully coated; no dry spots.

steps.title: Roast (oven method)
instructions: Line a sheet pan (or place a wire rack over the pan for better browning).
Spread chicken pieces out so they’re not crowded. Add optional sliced onion around the chicken.
Roast until chicken reaches 165°F internal temp (thighs can go to 175°F for best texture).

temperature.notes: High heat encourages browning like a rotisserie edge.

checks: Internal temp at thickest part is at least 165°F.
Edges are browned; juices run clear.

steps.title: Optional: Broil for shawarma-style char
instructions: Broil 1–3 minutes to deepen color and add charred edges (watch closely).
checks: Edges are lightly charred but not burnt.

steps.title: Rest and slice
instructions: Rest chicken 5–10 minutes so juices redistribute.
Optional: For extra street-style texture, quickly sear sliced chicken in a hot skillet for 1–2 minutes.
checks: If seared, edges are crisp with tender centers.

steps.title: Serve as wraps, bowls, or plates
instructions: Wrap: Spread toum (or garlic mayo) on warm pita/flatbread, add chicken, pickles/pickled turnips, tomatoes, and cucumber.
Bowl: Serve over rice or fries with chopped salad and sauce.
Plate: Serve with roasted vegetables and a bright salad.
checks: Balance is creamy + bright + savory; add lemon/pickles if it needs lift.

refrigerate.notes: Store sliced chicken in an airtight container. Reheat in a hot skillet for best texture.

freeze.notes: Freeze cooked sliced chicken in flat bags. Reheat from thawed for best results.

weeknightTips: Marinate the night before; roast when you get home.
Double batch and freeze half for fast wraps later.
scalingNotes: Do not overcrowd the pan; use two pans if doubling or you’ll steam instead of brown.

notes: Ingredient ids are SSA-style and may be mapped by your ingredient-alias system if needed.
If using chicken breast: roast closer to 18–22 minutes and pull at 160–165°F to prevent dryness.
If yogurt is very thick (Greek), loosen with 1–2 tbsp water so it coats evenly.
For extra authentic ‘shawarma shop’ flavor, add 1 tsp ground sumac or 1/2 tsp ground cloves (very small amount).
Air fry at 400°F for 12–16 minutes, turning halfway, until 165°F internal.
Grill thighs over medium-high heat 5–7 minutes per side until 165°F+.
Rest and slice thinly; finish with a squeeze of lemon.
Use unsweetened dairy-free yogurt (coconut or cashew).
If yogurt substitute is thin, reduce lemon slightly and add 1 tbsp olive oil to keep richness.
Increase cayenne to 1 tsp and add 1 tsp crushed red pepper.
Serve with a hot sauce or harissa-style mayo.
Marinate chicken in the refrigerator, not at room temperature.
Cook chicken to at least 165°F internal temperature; thighs are best around 175°F for tenderness.
Discard leftover marinade that touched raw chicken unless it is boiled.
